model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed budget of 256 optimizer steps, the current run is strongly sample-limited. Doubling batch size is a simple way to expose the model to twice as many training examples within the same step budget without adding architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current setup is now using the fixed step budget much more effectively after increasing batch size, so the next bottleneck is likely model capacity. A modest width increase in both the convolutional trunk and MLP head is a simple way to test whether the budget can support a slightly stronger compact CNN without a major architectural rewrite.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The width increase slightly hurt, which suggests the current budget is still better spent on sample throughput than extra capacity. Reverting to the stronger smaller model and testing an even larger batch directly probes whether more examples per fixed optimization step can push validation loss lower.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The larger batch is clearly helping in the fixed-step regime, which usually supports a higher stable learning rate. Doubling the learning rate is a simple next test that may convert the extra sample throughput into faster optimization progress without changing the model or loop structure.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The main wins so far have come from increasing how many examples are consumed within the fixed 256-step budget. Testing batch size 512 continues that pattern directly and may further improve loss by turning the step-limited regime into roughly 2.6 passes over the training set.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The run continues to improve as more data are processed per fixed step, and the larger batch typically supports a larger stable learning rate. A modest increase from 2e-3 to 3e-3 is a clean next test for faster optimization without changing compute cost or model structure.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The higher learning rate slightly overshot, so this backs out that change and tests a milder reduction in regularization instead. In a short fixed-step regime with no label noise, slightly less weight decay can help the model fit faster without introducing extra architectural or compute complexity.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimization side now looks reasonably tuned for the fixed-step regime, so the next useful probe is a slightly deeper feature extractor. Increasing depth from 2 to 3 adds one more pooling block and nonlinear stage while keeping the overall script simple and the final classifier size modest.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Depth 3 produced a large jump, which suggests the model was still representation-limited. A modest increase in base width is the cleanest next architecture test: it strengthens every stage of the now-effective deeper stack without changing the overall training recipe.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The deeper and wider convolutional stack is working well, and the remaining bottleneck may be in the small MLP head. Increasing the hidden size is a low-risk capacity bump that mostly affects the cheap classifier rather than the already expensive convolutional trunk.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The strongest gains are still coming from extra model capacity, and the current depth-3 configuration appears to optimize well under the existing training recipe. A moderate width increase from 16 to 20 is the next clean architecture probe without changing the overall design or introducing new training logic.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 4","old":"DEPTH = 3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The biggest improvement so far came from adding a third convolutional stage, which suggests the model is still benefiting from extra hierarchy more than from optimizer tweaks. Moving from depth 3 to 4 is the next clean capacity test and also keeps the classifier compact by reducing the final spatial size further.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The depth-4 trunk appears to be extracting much stronger features, and its final flattened representation is still fairly compact. Expanding the classifier hidden size is a cheap way to let the stronger backbone translate those features into logits more effectively without materially increasing convolutional cost.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The model is now much stronger and still trains for only a small number of effective epochs, so regularization may be holding back fit more than it is helping generalization. Removing weight decay is a simple test of whether the current fixed-step regime benefits from less constraint on the larger network.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Capacity increases have been the most reliable source of improvement in this run, and the current depth-4 model is still improving with only modest regularization. A moderate width increase from 20 to 24 is the cleanest next test to strengthen all stages of the backbone while keeping the rest of the training recipe unchanged.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current depth-4, wider backbone continues to benefit from added capacity, and the classifier remains relatively cheap compared with the convolutional stack. Increasing the hidden layer from 96 to 128 is a targeted way to capture more value from the stronger features without another large increase in convolutional cost.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 160","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The recent classifier-width increases have remained positive, while they are much cheaper than further widening the whole convolutional trunk. With only a few steps left, pushing the head a bit further is a focused way to test whether the stronger depth-4 backbone is still bottlenecked by classifier capacity.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The model has become substantially larger, and recent capacity increases are still helping, so the current learning rate may now be slightly aggressive for best validation loss. A modest reduction to 1.5e-3 is a targeted final-stage tuning move that may improve how well the stronger network settles within the same fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 28","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The lower learning rate moved slightly in the wrong direction, so this restores the stronger 2e-3 setting and uses the final step on the most reliable remaining lever: more backbone capacity. Width increases have been consistently productive in the depth-4 regime, and a moderate bump from 24 to 28 is a plausible final improvement while preserving the same overall design.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}
